summary: Full backend regression + one representative frontend E2E flow. Backend: 24/24 pytest cases pass covering auth (JWT + role gating), Projects CRUD, Import schema/upload/commit/summary, Master data CRUD + search + bulk-delete, Validation (can_generate flip), Timetable generate v1/v2 (versioned, never overwrite), entry toggle-lock, conflicts, and Exports (8 report keys, xlsx/csv content-type, pdf -> 400). Frontend: login (admin) -> Projects page -> New Project modal -> lands on Import Dashboard with 12 dataset cards -> Import Wizard for teachers (upload -> auto column mapping -> preview -> commit) -> success screen showed 3 imported / 0 errors -> dashboard card updated to '3 records / IMPORTED'. Screenshots captured at /app/test_reports/fe_final.png.

issues: Last imported timestamp rendered as '7/7/2026, 9:14:55 AM' via toLocaleString - locale/timezone appears odd (server tz vs display). Cosmetic only; verify with product owner if a fixed en-GB / ISO format is preferred.

critical_code_review_comments: auth.py imports `verify_password` inside `seed_default_users` which re-hashes when password changed; good idempotent seed. But it does not update `name` if the seed name changes - minor.

exports router: `/reports/list` is defined BEFORE `/{timetable_id}/export` under same prefix so FastAPI path resolution is fine, but a request to `/api/projects/{pid}/timetables/reports/list` returns the static list even though `project_id` in path is not validated. Consider verifying project existence to return 404 for missing project (currently just returns the same static list for any project_id).
importer.py `commit_import`: the row_num variable is initialised inside try, so if the very first row raises before iteration (edge case), row_num would be unbound. Minor.
exporter.render for xlsx uses report[:31] for sheet name - correct Excel limit, good.
importer.commit route accepts `Dict[str, Any]` payload rather than Pydantic model - loses validation. Consider a schema.
master_data.create_record: catches only TypeError; SQLAlchemy IntegrityError (duplicate code, FK violation) will bubble up as 500 instead of 400. Recommend wrapping in try/except IntegrityError.

Projects.js create() posts `year` field via `academic_year` correctly; but my backend test intentionally posts `year` (extra field) - Pydantic default `model_config` allows extras silently. Consider `model_config = ConfigDict(extra='forbid')` on ProjectIn for stricter validation.
auth.py: JWT tokens do NOT include a jti - no revocation/blacklist. Fine for MVP.
CORS_ORIGINS defaults to '*' with allow_credentials=True which browsers reject - restrict origins for production.
test_credentials: admin@timetable.app/admin123, planner@timetable.app/planner123, viewer@timetable.app/viewer123 - all validated working
seed_data_creation: Backend tests create ephemeral TEST_ projects and subjects; not cleaned up explicitly since project lifecycle test deletes its own. Frontend test creates 'TEST_FE_Project' with 3 teachers imported - safe to keep.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py must be run with `-n 0` because pytest.ini enforces xdist load_scope and tests share state via a session-scope `project_id` fixture and `pytest.tt_id` attribute set by TestTimetables. Sample xlsx files at /tmp/{teachers,subjects,classes,teacher_mapping}.xlsx are the golden inputs used by both backend commit tests and the frontend wizard test. If tests are re-run repeatedly, existing TEST_ projects will accumulate but should not affect correctness because each run creates a fresh uuid-suffixed project. All 24 backend tests pass in ~6s.
rca of the issue: No product issues found. My initial playwright assertion `'Imported' in text` failed because CSS transforms the chip label to uppercase and inner_text() preserves that as 'IMPORTED'. Confirmed via screenshot that the product flow works end-to-end.
